stemHtml: Which of the following answers is correct regarding the formation of ice on a propeller?

options.a: To counter icing conditions in a light aeroplane without de-icing, the pilot should reduce propeller RPM as far as possible.
options.b: Considering the whole aeroplane, the most critical effect of icing conditions is through ice formation on the propeller blades.
options.c: The presence of ice on propeller blades causes propeller efficiency to be reduced by up to 20%.
options.d: On a propeller blade, ice tends to form first towards the tip, due to the sharp leading edge.
